exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing `partition()` helper already has the correct conditional: when `record.partition() != null`, it returns early before reaching the `this.partitioner.partition()` call. Only the else-branch that calls `this.partitioner.partition()` needs the increment.
exploration_log.hindsight: Check `clear()` to see if it resets other counters — could consider resetting there too, but task doesn't ask for it.

alternatives: Increment at top of partition() method before null check
Add a separate tracking wrapper Partitioner
key_decisions.decision: Increment immediately before delegating to partitioner.partition() in the else-branch
key_decisions.reasoning: Only increment when partitioner is actually consulted — matches the task requirement exactly.

search_sequence.reasoning: Understand the flow to find the exact increment point
search_sequence.result: Confirmed: when record.partition() != null, returns early. When partitioner == null, uses default. Only when both conditions are false does partitioner.partition() get called.

queries.query_text: Add partitionerCallCount field and resetPartitionerCallCount() accessor to MockProducer, increment in private partition() helper when partitioner is consulted

solve_notes: Added an `int partitionerCallCount` field to `MockProducer`, incremented it inside the private `partition()` helper just before delegating to `this.partitioner.partition()` (only when `record.partition() == null` and partitioner is non-null), and added public `partitionerCallCount()` and `resetPartitionerCallCount()` methods.
task_text: Add an `int` field `partitionerCallCount` to `MockProducer`, increment it inside the `partition()` helper each time the partitioner is consulted (i.e., when `record.partition() == null` and a non-null `Partitioner` is set), and expose it via a public `partitionerCallCount()` accessor method. Also add a `resetPartitionerCallCount()` method so tests can reset the counter between successive assertions in the same test instance.

tier_difference_reasoning: The `partition()` method is both the only thing edited and the only context needed. No additional defs are required to avoid thrash — the method is self-contained and the increment location is obvious once you read it.
